chunks.section_title: Trading Questions and Answers | Help Centre | Deriv
chunks.text:
Trading
Is it normal to see candle timing differences across trading platforms? Yes, this is standard across the trading industry. Different platform providers use different timing systems, and this variation is normal when using multiple trading platforms. What should I do if I use both Deriv MT5 and cTrader platforms? Be aware that 4-hour chart patterns may look different between MT5 and cTrader due to the different candle start times. This is normal and expected. For consistency in your technical analysis, consider focusing on:
- Lower timeframes (1 hour and below) where platforms align
- One primary platform for your main chart analysis
Which instruments are affected by the candle timing difference? This timing difference affects all instruments available on both platforms, including:
- Volatility indices (Vol 10, Vol 75, etc.)
- Synthetic indices (Boom 1000, Crash, etc.)
- Forex pairs (EUR/USD, etc.)
How does the candle timing difference impact my technical analysis? The different candle timing can affect:
- Visual technical analysis on 4-hour charts
- Pattern recognition on higher timeframes
- Chart-based trading decisions when switching between platforms
The underlying market data is the same - only how it’s grouped and displayed differs. Does the candle timing difference affect all timeframes? No.

chunks.section_title: Trading Questions and Answers | Help Centre | Deriv
chunks.text:
The timing difference primarily impacts higher timeframes like the 4-hour charts. 1-hour timeframes and below generally align between platforms and show nearly identical candles, as the starting times match. Why do my charts look different between Deriv MT5 and cTrader? The main difference is due to different candle start times between the platforms:
- MT5: 4-hour candles start at 00:00 GMT (new candles at 00:00, 04:00, 08:00, 12:00, 16:00, 20:00)
- cTrader: 4-hour candles start at 01:00 GMT (new candles at 01:00, 05:00, 09:00, 13:00, 17:00, 21:00)
What this means: The same market data gets grouped into different 4-hour periods, creating different candle shapes and patterns between the platforms. How can I automate my trading strategy? Use a trading bot to automate your trading strategy. A trading bot is an automated computer program that purchases trade contracts for you while following a specific set of instructions that you provide. Build your trading bot for free on Deriv Bot; no coding is needed.

chunks.section_title: Trading Questions and Answers | Help Centre | Deriv
chunks.text:
You’ll also find 3 free pre-built strategies on Deriv Bot that you can customise to your needs. What are the trading limits on my account? To see your account’s trading limits, go to Settings > Security and safety > Account limits. What assets are available for trading on weekends? Synthetic indices and cryptocurrencies are available for trading 24/7. What are multipliers? Deriv multipliers combine the upside of leverage trading without the downside of losing more than your stake. This means that when the market moves in your favour, you'll multiply your potential profits. If the market moves against your prediction, your losses are limited only to your stake. To find out more, visit the Options page. How do I trade multipliers? Watch this video to know more about trading multipliers. How do I calculate the margin required to open a CFDs position? To calculate margin required to open a CFDs position, use the following formula:
Required Margin USD = Volume in USD ÷ Effective Leverage*
* Use the denominator of the Effective Leverage ratio, 1 : XXXX.

chunks.section_title: Trading Questions and Answers | Help Centre | Deriv
chunks.text:
To calculate the Volume in USD, use the following formula:
Forex: Lots x Contract size x Conversion Rate Base currency* to USD
Other: Lots x Contract size x Execution Price x [Conversion Rate of Base currency* to USD]
*in Deriv MT5, this is called “Margin Currency”. You'll be able to see Margin Currency in the instrument specification table in your trading terminal. How do I calculate the swap charges required to keep my CFDs positions open overnight? To calculate the swap charges required for overnight CFDs positions, use the following formulas:
For swaps in points:
Volume in lots x Contract size x Point Size* x Swap Rate
*Point Size = 1/10^digits. You can find the digits value in the instrument specification table in your trading terminal. For swaps in percentage:
(Volume in lots x Contract Size x Rollover Price*) x (Swap Rate ÷ 100) ÷ 360
*Rollover Price = The last price of the day before rollover is processed
How do I calculate the pip value of my trades?

chunks.section_title: Trading Questions and Answers | Help Centre | Deriv
chunks.text:
To calculate the pip value of your trades, use the following formula:
Pip value USD: Pip Size* x Lots x Contract Size ÷ Conversion rate of account currency to USD
*Pip size:
- Forex - non JPY pairs = 0.0001
- Forex - JPY pairs = 0.01
- Other = 1/10^digits
For instruments without weekend swaps, a triple swap is applied on one designated weekday. Please check your trading terminal for specific details. How many trading platforms do you offer? We have a diverse suite of 8 trading platforms: Deriv MT5, Deriv Nakala, Deriv cTrader, Deriv Trader, Deriv Bot, Deriv GO, and SmartTrader. Each platform is designed to fit any trading style, regardless of your trading experience. What is forex? Foreign exchange, or forex, is the global market of the world’s currencies, where the values of different currencies are pitted against each other in the form of forex pairs, such as EUR/USD, AUD/JPY, etc. The forex market determines the exchange rates of each currency. Read this article to learn more about trading forex on Deriv. What are commodities?

chunks.section_title: Trading Questions and Answers | Help Centre | Deriv
chunks.text: A commodity is either grown or produced naturally in the environment, such as agricultural products, livestock, crude oil, and precious metals like gold and silver. Read this article to learn more about trading commodities on Deriv. What are stock indices? Stock indices measure the value of a group of companies in the stock market. This allows investors to see how a particular set of assets is performing. Read this article to learn more about trading stocks on Deriv. What are derived indices? Derived indices consist of asset prices generated from real-world and simulated markets and indices, with little to no influence from real-world events. You can trade from a variety of derived indices, including synthetic indices, derived FX indices, tactical indices and baskets. Available 24/7, our synthetic indices emulate price movements of real-world markets with varying levels of volatility. As they aren't based on actual underlying assets, they are unaffected by real-world market events. Read this article to learn more about trading synthetic indices on Deriv. Derived FX indices are simulated assets with prices derived from the price movements of real major forex pairs. Our algorithms track real-world currency prices and dampen fluctuations caused by news events and market sentiment. Plus, you can choose to trade them at the volatility you prefer.

chunks.section_title: Trading Questions and Answers | Help Centre | Deriv
chunks.text: Tactical indices are simulated instruments designed to capture short-term market trends using signals from popular technical indicators. They provide structured price action that reflects common trading strategies and are unaffected by real-world market events. With basket indices, you can trade your favourite asset against a basket of five major global currencies, each weighted by 20%. Due to regulatory requirements, derived indices are unavailable in some countries. Refer to ‘Product offering’ in our terms of use for more information. What are contracts for difference (CFDs)? CFDs let you predict the price movement of underlying assets without actually owning them. With CFDs, you open a position based on your prediction, and you'll earn a profit if you close your position when the price moves in your favour. Read this article to learn more about trading CFDs on Deriv. Where can I get more help? If you have specific questions about chart differences or need clarification about how this affects your trading strategy, please contact our support team.

chunks.section_title: How to close your account | What happens to your open positions | What happens to your funds
chunks.text:
## How to close your account
You can close your Deriv account by contacting customer support through Live Chat or by emailing support. State that you wish to close your account and confirm your identity using the registered email address.

## What happens to your open positions
When you request to close your Deriv account, your open positions are not automatically closed by Deriv. You must close all open trades on every linked trading platform — Deriv MT5, Deriv X, Deriv cTrader, and any options or multipliers contracts on Deriv.com — before account closure can complete.

If you submit a closure request while positions are still open, support will respond asking you to close the positions first. The account closure request will be paused until you do. Deriv staff will not close trades on your behalf, and the platform will not force-close them; this avoids any disputes about exit prices.

## What happens to your funds
Before the account is closed, you must withdraw any remaining balance from your Deriv account using a supported withdrawal method. Any unclaimed balance below the minimum withdrawal amount can be donated to charity (Deriv has a partnership with Brick by Brick) or, in limited cases, refunded by support.

chunks.section_title: Reopening a closed account | Data retention
chunks.text:
## Reopening a closed account
A closed account can sometimes be reopened by contacting support, provided the closure was not for compliance reasons. If the closure was triggered by a compliance review, the decision is final and a new account cannot be opened.

## Data retention
Even after closure, Deriv retains your account records for a period required by financial regulations (typically 5 to 7 years depending on jurisdiction). This is for audit and anti-money-laundering compliance, not for marketing.

chunks.section_title: How many real accounts can I have | Account types
chunks.text:
## How many real accounts can I have
You can hold more than one real account on Deriv. In fact, most clients have several: a Deriv real account holds your fiat balance and is used for funding, while separate trading accounts (Deriv MT5, Deriv X, and Deriv cTrader) are used to trade specific markets.

You can also open additional Deriv real accounts in different fiat currencies (for example one in USD and one in EUR) and one cryptocurrency account per supported coin. This lets you keep balances in the currencies you trade with.

## Account types
- Deriv real account: holds funds and is used to fund all trading platforms.
- Deriv MT5: trades CFDs on forex, stocks, indices, commodities, cryptocurrencies, and Deriv synthetic indices.
- Deriv X: trades CFDs on the same asset classes through Deriv's own platform.
- Deriv cTrader: trades CFDs through the cTrader platform.

chunks.section_title: How to open additional accounts | Limits | Managing multiple accounts
chunks.text:
## How to open additional accounts
Go to Account settings, choose Add account, and select the account type and currency you want. Some account types may require additional verification depending on your country of residence and the regulator that oversees that account.

## Limits
Deriv does not permit duplicate accounts of the same type and currency for a single client. If you try to open a second account of the same type and currency, the request will be declined. If you need to change the currency of an existing account, contact support — currency changes are only allowed before the account has been funded.

## Managing multiple accounts
You can switch between accounts using the account selector in the top right of the Deriv interface. Internal transfers between your own accounts are typically instant and have no fee, except for currency conversion which is applied at the prevailing market rate.

chunks.section_title: Why verification is required | Documents required for clients in Malaysia
chunks.text:
## Why verification is required
Account verification (also called Know-Your-Customer or KYC) is required to comply with anti-money-laundering regulations and to keep your funds safe. Verification must usually be completed before your first withdrawal, though you can deposit and trade smaller amounts before verifying.

## Documents required for clients in Malaysia
Clients with a Malaysian address must submit:

1. Proof of identity — a clear scan or photo of one of:
 - Malaysian national identity card (MyKad), front and back
 - Valid Malaysian passport (photo page)
 - Malaysian driving licence

2. Proof of address — a document, less than 3 months old, showing your full name and current Malaysian residential address. Acceptable documents include:
 - Utility bill (electricity, water, gas, telephone, or internet)
 - Bank statement
 - Tax assessment notice from LHDN
 - Government correspondence

3. Selfie verification — a clear photo of you holding your identity document, used to confirm that the document belongs to you.

chunks.section_title: What is not accepted | How to upload documents | After verification
chunks.text:
## What is not accepted
Do not submit screenshots of mobile-banking apps, edited or marked-up scans, expired documents, or documents in someone else's name. Documents must be in colour and the entire page must be visible.

## How to upload documents
Go to Account settings, choose Verification, and upload each document under the corresponding category. Most submissions are reviewed within 1 to 2 business days. You will receive an email when verification completes or if additional information is needed.

## After verification
Once your documents are approved, your account is fully verified. You can then withdraw funds without restrictions and access higher trading limits. You only need to verify once unless your documents expire or your address changes — in which case Deriv may ask for an updated document.

chunks.section_title: Supported cryptocurrencies | Are there fees for crypto deposits? | Minimum deposit amount
chunks.text:
## Supported cryptocurrencies
Deriv supports deposits in Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Tether (USDT — ERC-20 and TRC-20), Litecoin (LTC), Bitcoin Cash (BCH), and several other major cryptocurrencies. The exact list available to you depends on your country of residence.

## Are there fees for crypto deposits?
Deriv does not charge a deposit fee for cryptocurrency deposits. However, you will pay the standard blockchain network fee (the "gas" fee or miner fee) to the network when you broadcast the transaction from your wallet. This network fee is paid to the blockchain, not to Deriv, and varies with network congestion.

## Minimum deposit amount
The minimum cryptocurrency deposit amount varies by coin. As examples: BTC 0.0002, ETH 0.005, USDT 5, LTC 0.05. Below these minimums, the deposit will not be credited. Always check the current minimum on the Cashier page before sending.

chunks.section_title: Processing time | How to deposit | Conversion to fiat
chunks.text:
## Processing time
Crypto deposits are credited after a fixed number of network confirmations. For BTC this is typically 1 confirmation (about 10 to 30 minutes). For ETH and ERC-20 tokens, this is typically 12 confirmations (about 3 minutes). For TRC-20 USDT, this is typically 19 confirmations (under 1 minute). Once the required confirmations are reached, the deposit is credited automatically.

## How to deposit
Sign in, go to the Cashier, select Deposit, choose your cryptocurrency, and copy the deposit address (and memo/tag where applicable). Send the funds from your own wallet to that address. Do not deposit from an exchange or a wallet that is not in your own name — Deriv may freeze or return the deposit for compliance review.

## Conversion to fiat
Crypto deposits land in a Deriv crypto account in the same coin. To trade fiat-denominated CFDs you can transfer between your crypto and fiat Deriv accounts; the conversion rate is shown before you confirm.

chunks.section_title: What is 2FA | How to enable 2FA | How to reset 2FA if you lost access to your authenticator app
chunks.text:
## What is 2FA
Two-factor authentication adds a second layer of security to your Deriv account. After enabling 2FA, you will need to enter both your password and a six-digit code from an authenticator app (such as Google Authenticator or Authy) every time you sign in.

## How to enable 2FA
Go to Settings, select Two-factor authentication, scan the QR code with your authenticator app, and enter the six-digit code shown by the app to confirm. Once confirmed, 2FA is active on your account and will be required at every sign-in.

## How to reset 2FA if you lost access to your authenticator app
If you no longer have access to your authenticator app — for example because you lost your device, switched phones, or uninstalled the app — you can reset 2FA by contacting Deriv customer support through Live Chat. Support will verify your identity using your registered email address, your account number, and a copy of your government-issued ID. Once your identity is confirmed, support will disable 2FA on your account so you can sign in with your password and re-enable 2FA on a new device.

For your security, the reset request can take up to 48 hours to process. During this time, your account will remain locked from new sign-ins. Make sure to keep your registered email address up to date so support can reach you.

chunks.section_title: Backup codes | Best practices
chunks.text:
## Backup codes
When you first enable 2FA, Deriv displays a list of one-time backup codes. Save these codes in a secure location. Each backup code can be used once in place of the authenticator app code if you cannot access your device. Using a backup code does not disable 2FA — it just lets you sign in for that session.

## Best practices
Use a dedicated authenticator app rather than SMS where possible. Keep your backup codes printed or stored in an offline password manager. Do not share your authenticator codes or backup codes with anyone, including Deriv support — Deriv staff will never ask for these codes.

chunks.section_title: Why set limits | How to set trading limits | Self-exclusion
chunks.text:
## Why set limits
Deriv provides self-imposed limits as part of its responsible trading and responsible-gambling tools. Setting limits in advance helps you keep your trading aligned with what you can afford and prevents emotional decision-making during volatile market conditions.

## How to set trading limits
Go to Account settings, choose Self-exclusion, and select the limit type you want to set. The available limit categories are:

- Maximum total stake per day, week, or month
- Maximum total loss per day, week, or month
- Maximum open positions
- Maximum account balance
- Session time reminder

Once you set a limit, it becomes effective immediately. Limits can be lowered (made stricter) at any time and the change is immediate. Limits can only be raised (made looser) after a 24-hour cooling-off period — this delay is intentional and cannot be skipped.

## Self-exclusion
You can also exclude yourself from trading entirely for a fixed period (between 6 months and 5 years) or permanently. Once a self-exclusion is active, you cannot sign in to place trades during that period. Self-exclusion cannot be reversed before its end date.

chunks.section_title: Reality checks | Where to get help
chunks.text:
## Reality checks
Reality-check pop-ups can be configured to remind you, every 10 to 60 minutes, how long you have been trading and the running profit or loss for the session. The reminder requires you to acknowledge it before continuing.

## Where to get help
If you feel that your trading has become harmful, contact the support organisations listed in the Help Centre, such as GamCare or BeGambleAware. Deriv support can also help you set up a self-exclusion if you do not want to do it yourself.

chunks.section_title: Markets and instruments | Platforms | Account currencies | Margin and leverage
chunks.text:
## Markets and instruments
Deriv offers trading on forex, stocks and stock indices, commodities, cryptocurrencies, and Deriv-only synthetic indices that are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week. The exact instrument list depends on the regulator that oversees your account.

## Platforms
Trading is available on three main platforms:
- Deriv MT5 (MetaTrader 5)
- Deriv X (Deriv's own platform)
- Deriv cTrader

In addition, Deriv.com offers options and multipliers products through a web interface that does not require any installation.

## Account currencies
Trading accounts can be denominated in fiat currencies (USD, EUR, GBP, AUD, etc.) or in cryptocurrencies (BTC, ETH, USDT, LTC, BCH). The currency is selected when the account is created and cannot normally be changed afterwards. Funding a trading account from a Deriv real account in a different currency triggers a conversion at the prevailing market rate.

## Margin and leverage
Leverage on Deriv MT5 depends on the asset class and the regulator that oversees your account. Typical maximums are 1:1000 on forex majors, 1:300 on stock indices and commodities, and 1:30 on cryptocurrencies. Lower leverage applies for accounts under EU regulators. The exact leverage for each asset is shown on the contract details page.

chunks.section_title: Spreads and commissions | Risk warning
chunks.text:
## Spreads and commissions
Most Deriv MT5 accounts use spread-only pricing with no separate commission. Some account types (for example the "Zero spread" account) use a tighter spread plus a commission per lot. The applicable spread and commission for each instrument are shown on the platform.

## Risk warning
Trading CFDs involves significant risk and may not be suitable for all investors. You can lose more than your initial deposit. Always trade with money you can afford to lose, and consider using the self-imposed limits tools described in the Responsible trading help page.

chunks.section_title: Minimum and maximum withdrawal amounts | Processing time | Fees | How to request a bank transfer withdrawal
chunks.text:
## Minimum and maximum withdrawal amounts
The minimum withdrawal amount for bank transfer is USD 10 (or equivalent in your account currency). The maximum withdrawal amount per transaction is USD 10,000. There is no monthly cap, but very large withdrawals may be split into multiple transactions for compliance purposes.

## Processing time
Bank transfer withdrawals are typically processed within 1 to 3 business days on the Deriv side. After Deriv has approved and sent the withdrawal, your bank may take an additional 2 to 5 business days to credit the funds to your account, depending on your country and your bank's processing speed. International wire transfers may take longer.

## Fees
Deriv does not charge a fee for bank transfer withdrawals. However, intermediary banks or your receiving bank may charge a fee that is deducted from the transferred amount. For SWIFT transfers, the typical intermediary fee ranges between USD 15 and USD 35.

## How to request a bank transfer withdrawal
Sign in to your Deriv account, go to the Cashier, choose Withdraw, and select Bank transfer. Enter the amount, your bank account details (IBAN or account number, SWIFT/BIC code, bank name and address), and confirm. You may be asked to verify your identity before the first withdrawal of a given method.

chunks.section_title: Currency conversion
chunks.text:
## Currency conversion
If your bank account currency differs from your Deriv account currency, the conversion is performed by your bank at their prevailing exchange rate. Deriv displays the requested amount in your account currency only.

chunks.section_title: Supported e-wallets | Processing time | Minimum and maximum amounts | Fees
chunks.text:
## Supported e-wallets
Deriv supports a range of e-wallets for withdrawals, including Skrill, Neteller, WebMoney, Perfect Money, Sticpay, and AstroPay. Availability depends on your country of residence.

## Processing time
E-wallet withdrawals are typically the fastest method on Deriv. Most e-wallet withdrawals are processed within 24 hours by Deriv. After Deriv approves the request, the funds usually arrive in your e-wallet account within minutes — often instantly. In total, you should expect an e-wallet withdrawal to complete within 1 business day from the time you submit the request.

## Minimum and maximum amounts
The minimum e-wallet withdrawal amount is USD 5. The maximum per transaction varies by provider, typically between USD 5,000 and USD 10,000.

## Fees
Deriv does not charge a fee for e-wallet withdrawals. The e-wallet provider may charge their own fee — please check your e-wallet's terms.

chunks.section_title: Requirements | Troubleshooting delayed withdrawals
chunks.text:
## Requirements
Your e-wallet account must be in your own name. Withdrawals can only be sent to an e-wallet that has previously been used to deposit funds into your Deriv account, or to an e-wallet that you have separately verified with Deriv. This is a regulatory requirement to prevent fraud.

## Troubleshooting delayed withdrawals
If your e-wallet withdrawal has not arrived after 24 hours, check your Deriv withdrawal history. If the status is "Processed", contact your e-wallet provider with the reference number. If the status is "In review", Deriv compliance may need to verify your identity — they will reach you by email within 1 to 2 business days.
